figures.caption: Table 2: Object detector-level mAP of PULSAR-Net (Ours) and baselines on the nuScenes-J and KITTI-J datasets. ϕ denotes the number of simultaneous sensing lasers.

Fig. 1: Reconstruction demo of our PULSAR-Net from LiDAR sensing under a jamming attack. Our PULSAR-Net can effectively recover the point cloud (d), which is faithful to the original one (b), by leveraging the intermediate full-waveform representation and simultaneous sensing feature available in modern LiDARs.

Fig. 3: Overview of PULSAR-Net, a U-Net-inspired architecture using depthwiseseparable 3D convolutions for efficient, lightweight feature extraction. It incorporates axial attention to model both spatial and temporal dependencies within full-waveforms.

Table 1: Point-level recovery accuracy of PULSAR-Net (Ours) and the baselines (Naive Average Subtraction and Neural DSP) on the nuScenes-J and KITTI-J datasets. ϕ is the number of simultaneous sensing lasers.

Fig. 4: Overview of the real-world experiment and its results: (i) experimental setup of a jamming attack on our LiDAR developed with a major LiDAR supplier to stream full waveform, (ii) benign, attack-compromised, and reconstructed point clouds in a static scenario with a reference vision, and (iii-iv) 3 frames of camera frames and point clouds of benign, attack-compromised, and reconstructed point clouds in driving scenarios
Table 10: Object detector-level mAP of PULSAR-Net (Ours) and baselines on the nuScenes-J and KITTI-J datasets. ϕ denotes the number of simultaneous sensing lasers.
